heading: Authorities
label: 19116 Authorities
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Par. (7) of amended .section 10396 of Pub. L. 117–167section 10396 of Pub. L. 117–167section 1870 of this title
amendments: CodificationSection is comprised of . Par. (7) of amended .section 10396 of Pub. L. 117–167section 10396 of Pub. L. 117–167section 1870 of this title

children.heading: Awards
children.label: 1 Awards
children.text: In carrying out this part, the Director may provide awards in the form of grants, contracts, cooperative agreements, cash prizes, and other transactions.

children.label: i
children.text: have expertise in one or more of the challenges and key technology focus areas under ; andsection 19107 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: come from a variety of backgrounds, including industry, and from a variety of institutions of higher education.

children.label: i
children.text: establishing research and development goals for the program, including through the convening of workshops, conferring with a broad range of stakeholders and outside experts, taking into account relevant expert reports, and publicizing the goals of the program to the public and private sectors;

children.label: ii
children.text: surveying a wide range of institutions of higher education, nonprofit organizations, and private entities to identify emerging trends in the challenges and key technology focus areas under , and, as appropriate, soliciting proposals from such entities to conduct research in areas of particular promise that the private sector is the not likely to undertake independently.section 19107 of this title1So in original. The period probably should be a semicolon.1

children.label: iii
children.text: facilitating research collaborations in the challenges and key technology focus areas under , including connecting academic researchers with potential end-users of technology, including industry, labor organizations, nonprofit organizations, civil society organizations, and other relevant organizations;section 19107 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: reviewing applications for projects submitted under according to the Merit Review Criteria established by the Director for such projects and described in the Foundation’s Proposal and Award Policies and Procedures Guide, and any such additional criteria as determined by the Director; andsection 19114 of this title

children.label: v
children.text: monitoring the progress of projects supported under the program and taking into account input from relevant experts and stakeholders, recommending program updates as needed.

children.heading: Selection criteria
children.label: C Selection criteria
children.text: Program directors may use diverse merit review models for selection of award recipients under , including internal review and different models that use peer review.section 19114 of this title

children.heading: Terms
children.label: D Terms
children.text: Program directors of the Directorate may be appointed by the Director for a limited term, renewable at the discretion of the Director.

children.heading: Program authorized
children.label: A Program authorized
children.text: The Foundation may carry out a program of personnel management authority provided under subparagraph (B) in order to facilitate recruitment of eminent experts in science or engineering for research and development projects and to enhance the administration and management of the Foundation.

children.label: i
children.text: without regard to any provision of title 5 governing the appointment of employees in the competitive service, appoint individuals to a total of not more than 70 positions in the Foundation, of which not more than 5 such positions may be positions of administration or management of the Foundation;

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of employees appointed pursuant to clause (i) to any of 5 positions designated by the Foundation for purposes of this clause, at rates not in excess of a rate equal to 150 percent of the maximum rate of basic pay authorized for positions at level I of the Executive Schedule under ; andsection 5312 of title 5

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any other employee appointed pursuant to clause (i), at rates not in excess of the maximum rate of basic pay authorized for senior-level positions under ; andsection 5376 of title 5

children.label: iii
children.text: pay any employee appointed under subparagraph (A), other than an employee appointed to a position designated as described in clause (ii)(I), payments in addition to basic pay within the limit applicable to the employee under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Limitation on term of appointment
children.label: C Limitation on term of appointment

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the service of an employee under an appointment under subparagraph (B)(i) may not exceed 4 years.

children.heading: Extension
children.label: ii Extension
children.text: The Director may, in the case of a particular employee under the program under subparagraph (A), extend the period to which service is limited under clause (i) by up to 2 years if the Director determines that such action is necessary to promote the efficiency of the Foundation.

children.heading: Maximum amount of additional payments payable
children.label: D Maximum amount of additional payments payable
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection or , no additional payments may be paid to an employee under subparagraph (B)(iii) in any calendar year if, or to the extent that, the employee’s total annual compensation in such calendar year will exceed the maximum amount of total annual compensation payable at the salary set in accordance with .section 5307 of title 5section 104 of title 3

children.heading: Highly qualified experts in needed occupations
children.label: 4 Highly qualified experts in needed occupations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Foundation may carry out a program using the authority provided in subparagraph (B) in order to attract highly qualified experts in needed occupations, as determined by the Foundation. Individuals hired by the Director through such authority may include individuals with expertise in business creativity, innovation management, design thinking, entrepreneurship, venture capital, and related fields.

children.label: i
children.text: appoint personnel from outside the civil service and uniformed services (as such terms are defined in ) to positions in the Foundation without regard to any provision of title 5 governing the appointment of employees in the competitive service;section 2101 of title 5

children.label: ii
children.text: prescribe the rates of basic pay for positions to which employees are appointed under clause (i) at rates not in excess of the maximum rate of basic pay authorized for senior-level positions under ; andsection 5376 of title 5

children.label: iii
children.text: pay any employee appointed under clause (i) payments in addition to basic pay within the limits applicable to the employee under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Limitation on term of appointment
children.label: C Limitation on term of appointment

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the service of an employee under an appointment made pursuant to this subsection may not exceed 5 years.

children.heading: Extension
children.label: ii Extension
children.text: The Foundation may, in the case of a particular employee, extend the period to which service is limited under clause (i) by up to 1 additional year if the Foundation determines that such action is necessary to promote the Foundation’s national security missions.

children.heading: Total amount
children.label: i Total amount
children.text: The total amount of the additional payments paid to an employee under this subsection for any 12-month period may not exceed the maximum amount of total compensation payable at the salary set in accordance with section 104 of title.2So in original. Probably should be “”.2section 104 of title 3

children.heading: Eligibility for payments
children.label: ii Eligibility for payments
children.text: An employee appointed under this subsection is not eligible for any bonus, monetary award, or other monetary incentive for service, except for payments authorized under this subsection.

children.heading: Limitation on number of highly qualified experts
children.label: E Limitation on number of highly qualified experts
children.text: The number of highly qualified experts appointed and retained by the Foundation under sub (B)(i) shall not exceed 70 at any time.3So in original. Probably should be “subparagraph”.3

children.label: I
children.text: the period for which the employee was appointed; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period to which the employee’s service is limited under subparagraph (C), including any extension made under this paragraph before the termination of the program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the rate of basic pay prescribed for the position under this paragraph may not be reduced as long as the employee continues to serve at an acceptable level of performance in the position without a break in service.

children.heading: Additional hiring authority
children.label: 5 Additional hiring authority
children.text: To the extent needed to carry out the duties under paragraph (1)(A), the Director is authorized to utilize hiring authorities under to staff the Foundation with employees from other Federal agencies, State and local governments, Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations, institutions of higher education, and other organizations, as described in that section, in the same manner and subject to the same conditions, that apply to such individuals utilized to accomplish other missions of the Foundation.section 3372 of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: evaluate and make recommendations to efficiently and effectively implement the Directorate for Technology, Innovation, and Partnerships; and

children.label: ii
children.text: evaluate and make recommendations to ensure coordination of the Directorate for Technology, Innovation, and Partnerships with other directorates and offices of the Foundation and other Federal agencies.

children.heading: Review
children.label: B Review
children.text: Upon completion of the study under subparagraph (A), the Foundation shall review the recommendations from the National Academy of Public Administration and provide a briefing to Congress on the plans of the Foundation to implement any such recommendations.
